UBER.whatItIs: The largest global ride-sharing and food delivery platform, now pivoting toward logistics and freight.
UBER.whyOnWatchlist: Generating real free cash flow for the first time while still growing top line. Mobility and delivery networks create a moat that's tough to replicate locally, let alone globally. Autonomous vehicle partnerships could flip the cost structure entirely.
UBER.whatTriggersBuy: Break above $76 on volume with RVOL kicking in, or news of exclusive AV deployment deal. Want to see driver supply costs stabilizing in next earnings. Bot status: add to existing position — Held, underweight (5.0%) and still eligible.
UBER.catalyst: Any AV partner selecting Uber as exclusive ride-hail fleet operator, or FCF guidance raised in Q2.
UBER.risk: Waymo, Tesla, or others roll out their own ride networks and cut Uber out. Driver pay inflation persists, squeezing take rates. Regulators reclassify gig workers as employees, breaking the model in key states.
UBER.alpacaNewsHeadline: 10 Industrials Stocks Whale Activity In Today’s Session

DUOL.whatTriggersBuy: Pullback to $115 area with volume drying up, or breakout above $130 on fresh AI product news. Need a better entry than chasing 17% in 20 days. Bot status: hold current position — Held at 4.8% — no add planned.

ETSY.whatItIs: Online marketplace connecting makers of handmade and vintage goods with buyers seeking unique items.
ETSY.whyOnWatchlist: GMV comparisons get easier in second half as pandemic surge fully anniversaries. Marketplace flywheel still spins: sellers attract buyers, buyers attract sellers. Valuation compressed to where a stabilization in growth looks attractive.
ETSY.whatTriggersBuy: Not chasing here. Need to see $80 cleared with MACD turning positive, or concrete evidence that active buyer decline is bottoming. Bot status: hold current position — Held at 4.6% — no add planned.
ETSY.catalyst: Seller fee structure changes stick without mass exodus, or international markets showing GMV acceleration.
ETSY.risk: Creators revolt over fee hikes and migrate to Shopify, Amazon Handmade, or TikTok Shop. Core buyer cohort ages out without Gen Z replacement. GMV keeps shrinking, proving the pandemic boost wasn't just pull-forward but peak demand.
ETSY.alpacaNewsHeadline: America's Child Care Crisis Could Cost The Economy $329 Billion— Why Congress Wants More Businesses To Use An Overlooked Tax Credit

SNOW.whatTriggersBuy: MACD crossing positive with RSI holding below 70; or guidance showing net revenue retention bottoming. Want confirmation that consumption isn't still decelerating. Bot status: add to existing position — Held, underweight (4.7%) and still eligible.

SNOW.risk: Customers keep optimizing spend rather than expanding. Databricks' lakehouse narrative wins over CIOs. NRR slide continues, proving the consumption model isn't as elastic as sold.

UPST.whatItIs: AI-driven lending marketplace connecting borrowers with bank and credit union partners for personal and auto loans.
UPST.whyOnWatchlist: Credit cycle fears already chewed the stock down 95% from highs, so asymmetry might be here. Neuberger financing deal buys runway. If unemployment stays benign, loan volumes and model performance could surprise.
UPST.whatTriggersBuy: Not yet. Need to see sustained move above VWAP with volume confirmation, or originations data showing personal loan demand snapback. MACD positive is start, not signal. Bot status: not yet ready to buy — Readiness 76.0 / 5 confirmations.
UPST.catalyst: Auto loan segment scaling faster than expected, or Fed cuts sparking refinancing wave that Upstart captures.
UPST.risk: Any recession smashes loan performance and partner confidence simultaneously. Funding partners pull back when defaults rise, starving volume. CFPB or DOJ scrutiny on AI lending models creates regulatory overhang regardless of economics.
UPST.alpacaNewsHeadline: Upstart Expected To Receive $600M Investment Under Renewed Agreement With Neuberger Specialty Finance

PINS.whatItIs: A visual discovery platform where users pin images and ideas, now layering shopping and ads into the feed.
PINS.whyOnWatchlist: Pinterest is riding the social commerce wave with an Amazon partnership that lets users buy without leaving the app. Ad budgets are recovering slowly, and Pinterest sits at the cheap end of digital ad CPMs.
PINS.whatTriggersBuy: Volume spike on a breakout above recent highs, or quarterly numbers showing the Amazon partnership actually converting to revenue, not just press releases. Bot status: hold current position — Held at 0.5% — no add planned.
PINS.catalyst: AI tools like the new scent scanner pulling brand ad dollars, or proof that Amazon partnership revenue is scaling faster than expected.
PINS.risk: User growth flatlined and nobody knows if it re-accelerates. The Amazon deal is early and could underwhelm. Ad markets turn fast in a slowdown, and Pinterest is small enough to get squeezed out by Meta and Google.

MDB.whatItIs: Document-based NoSQL database leader with managed cloud service Atlas driving nearly all growth.
MDB.whyOnWatchlist: Developer love is real and durable; MongoDB remains the default for modern application builds. Atlas growth still 30%+, which is rare air in infrastructure software. Stock washed out last year, creating setup if numbers hold.
MDB.whatTriggersBuy: Reclaim VWAP with MACD flipping positive, or any quarter where Atlas net new ARR surprises to upside. Below VWAP means institutions aren't accumulating yet. Bot status: hold current position — Held at 5.0% — no add planned.
MDB.catalyst: Vector search adoption for AI apps driving new workloads, or price increase on Atlas tiers sticking without churn spike.
MDB.risk: Atlas growth decelerates into the 20s, removing premium multiple justification. Snowflake and Databricks encroach on transactional-analytical convergence. Developer tastes shift to PostgreSQL or newer databases.
MDB.alpacaNewsHeadline: Software Stocks Are Sinking In 2026, But These 'Fab Five' Are Up 30% — And BofA Says They're Just Getting Started

SOFI.whatItIs: A digital bank offering student loans, personal loans, checking accounts, and back-end tech for other fintechs through its Galileo division.
SOFI.whyOnWatchlist: SoFi's finally got a bank charter, which cut funding costs and lets it hold deposits. The stock's been running on neobank momentum and any whiff of student loan refinancing coming back is gasoline.
SOFI.whatTriggersBuy: A pullback to EMA support with RSI cooling off, or concrete policy news on student loan refinancing that isn't just political theater. Bot status: not yet ready to buy — Readiness 74.8 / 5 confirmations.
SOFI.catalyst: Federal student loan refinancing reopening would flood SoFi's originations, or Galileo landing a major new platform customer.
SOFI.risk: Student loan policy is a political football and could stay frozen for years. Deposit costs keep rising and squeeze net interest margin. Galileo has customer concentration risk—lose one big fish and the narrative cracks.

HD.whatItIs: The dominant home improvement retailer in North America, selling everything from lumber to lawn mowers to contractors and weekend warriors.
HD.whyOnWatchlist: HD's been on a tear as rate-cut hopes stirred housing turnover fantasies, but RSI is pushing 70 and the move looks extended. Traders are watching for a blow-off or a graceful consolidation.
HD.whatTriggersBuy: A shallow pullback to rising EMA support with volume drying up, or hard data showing housing starts and existing home sales breaking out of their freeze. Bot status: hold current position — Held at 8.0% — no add planned.

HD.risk: Rates aren't cooperating and housing is stuck. The DIY boom from COVID was pulled forward and demand is normalizing uglier than expected. Lumber and commodity costs swing wild and can crush gross margins quarter to quarter.

NKE.whatItIs: The world's biggest sportswear brand, selling shoes and apparel through stores, wholesale partners, and its own apps and websites.
NKE.whyOnWatchlist: Nike's pushing hard to sell direct-to-consumer and cut out the middleman, which could juice margins if it works. China still moves the needle on revenue, so any demand flicker there gets traders watching.
NKE.whatTriggersBuy: A clean break above VWAP with volume stepping in, or any signal that China inventory is clearing and orders are picking back up. Bot status: hold current position — Held at 7.7% — no add planned.
NKE.catalyst: DTC sales growth re-accelerating or a China stimulus bump that gets foot traffic moving again in key cities.
NKE.risk: China's consumer is shaky and could stay that way. Inventory piled up last year and might need more discounting to clear. The DTC shift sounds good on paper but execution has been clunky and margins haven't followed through yet.

DDOG.whatItIs: Datadog sells software that monitors cloud servers, apps, and databases, charging customers based on how much they use it.
DDOG.whyOnWatchlist: Cloud spending is the debate—some companies are cutting, others are doubling down on AI infrastructure that needs monitoring. DDOG's usage model means revenue can swing fast in either direction.
DDOG.whatTriggersBuy: A volume-backed move through VWAP with MACD turning, or any survey data showing cloud optimization fatigue and spend re-acceleration. Bot status: hold current position — Held at 2.1% — no add planned.
DDOG.catalyst: AI workload monitoring becoming a standalone revenue line, or enterprise customers reversing course on cloud cost-cutting.
DDOG.risk: The usage-based model cuts both ways—when customers optimize cloud spend, DDOG revenue drops with no warning. Grafana is open-source and cheap, Splunk's got enterprise lock-in. The observability market is getting crowded and commoditized.
DDOG.alpacaNewsHeadline: $100 Invested In Datadog 5 Years Ago Would Be Worth This Much Today

SHOP.risk: SMBs fail in recessions—always have, always will. Amazon is getting more aggressive with Buy with Prime, and WooCommerce/Stripe keep chipping away at the low end. Take rate compression is a persistent threat.

ROKU.whatItIs: Streaming platform that sells cheap hardware to own the TV operating system and sell ads against it.
ROKU.whyOnWatchlist: Stock's been on a tear up 16% in 20 days with RSI pushing 76, so something's working. The Florida privacy settlement clearing a legal overhang helps, but this is really about whether the ad inventory story is finally clicking after years of pressure from Amazon and Google.
ROKU.whatTriggersBuy: Need to see volume actually show up to confirm this move isn't just thin-air momentum, and a clean break back above VWAP with RSI cooling off first. Entry eligible says no for a reason—chasing here gets you stuffed. Bot status: not yet ready to buy — Readiness 71.5 / 4 confirmations.
ROKU.catalyst: TV OEM partnerships keep expanding the OS footprint and the ad load per hour is still way below cable, so there's room to monetize the installed base harder without killing growth.
ROKU.risk: Smart TV makers like Samsung and LG keep their own platforms, Amazon's Fire OS is a constant price war threat, and the ad market's cyclical so any macro wobble hits Roku first. RSI at 76 says a lot of good news is already priced and a pullback could hurt.
ROKU.alpacaNewsHeadline: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier And Roku Announce Resolution Of Digital Privacy Enforcement Action
